title: Fundamental Commissioning and Verification
intent: Support the design, construction, and eventual operation of a project that meets the owner's project requirements for energy, water, indoor environmental quality, and durability.

requirements.notes: CxA must have documented commissioning process experience on at least two buildings; cannot be responsible for project design or construction management.
Develop OPR before the design phase; document energy, water, IEQ, and durability requirements.
Develop BOD documenting how the design meets the OPR; update as design progresses.

Develop and implement a commissioning plan covering mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and renewable energy systems. Include at minimum: HVAC&R, lighting controls, domestic hot water, and renewable energy systems.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, EA Prerequisite: Fundamental Commissioning and Verification

title: Minimum Energy Performance
intent: Reduce the environmental and economic harms of excessive energy use by establishing a minimum level of energy efficiency for the building and its systems.

requirements.notes: Demonstrate a 5% improvement in proposed building performance rating compared to the baseline building performance rating per ASHRAE 90.1-2010 Appendix G + cost budget method.

requirements.option: Prescriptive compliance (ASHRAE 50% Advanced Energy Design Guide)
requirements.notes: Alternative path: comply with the prescriptive measures in the applicable ASHRAE 50% Advanced Energy Design Guide for the building type.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, EA Prerequisite: Minimum Energy Performance

title: Outdoor Water Use Reduction

requirements.notes: Reduce outdoor water use by 30% from the calculated baseline for the site's peak watering month. Baseline uses mid-summer with no precipitation. No-landscaping or xeriscape projects that eliminate potable irrigation qualify automatically.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, WE Prerequisite: Outdoor Water Use Reduction

title: Indoor Water Use Reduction

requirements.notes: Reduce aggregate water consumption by 20% from the baseline. Baseline fixture performance: water closets 1.6 gpf, urinals 1.0 gpf, public lavatory faucets 0.5 gpm, private lavatory faucets 2.2 gpm, showerheads 2.5 gpm, kitchen faucets 2.2 gpm, pre-rinse spray valves 1.6 gpm.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, WE Prerequisite: Indoor Water Use Reduction

title: Minimum Indoor Air Quality Performance
intent: Contribute to the comfort and well-being of building occupants by establishing minimum standards for indoor air quality.

requirements.notes: Meet the minimum requirements of Sections 4 through 7 of ASHRAE 62.1-2010, Ventilation for Acceptable Indoor Air Quality. Ventilation Rate Procedure or IAQ Procedure may be used.
Meet the requirements of ASHRAE 62.1-2010 Section 4 for naturally ventilated spaces. Use the natural ventilation procedure from CIBSE AM10 or Chapter 16 of the ASHRAE Handbook of Fundamentals.
Provide exhaust as required by ASHRAE 62.1-2010 Table 6-4 for applicable space types.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, IEQ Prerequisite: Minimum Indoor Air Quality Performance

title: Optimize Energy Performance
intent: Achieve increasing levels of energy performance beyond the prerequisite standard to reduce environmental and economic harms associated with excessive energy use.

requirements.notes: Percentage improvement in proposed building performance rating compared to baseline per ASHRAE 90.1-2010 Appendix G. Major renovation thresholds are 3% lower at each tier.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, EA Credit: Optimize Energy Performance

title: Indoor Water Use Reduction

requirements.notes: Percentage reduction from the baseline calculated per EPAct 1992 fixture performance rates.
EPAct 1992 baseline rates. LEED v4.1 design case targets: toilets 1.28 gpf, urinals 0.5 gpf, lavatory faucets 0.5 gpm (public) or 1.5 gpm (private), kitchen faucets 2.2 gpm, showerheads 2.0 gpm.
Recommended efficient fixture rates to achieve indoor water use reduction. Using these rates in the design case helps meet the prerequisite and earn credit points.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, WE Credit: Indoor Water Use Reduction

title: Thermal Comfort
intent: Promote occupants' productivity, comfort, and well-being by providing quality thermal comfort.

requirements.notes: Design HVAC systems and the building envelope to meet ASHRAE 55-2010 requirements. PMV (Predicted Mean Vote) must be between -0.5 and +0.5 in regularly occupied spaces. Also meet PPD (Predicted Percentage Dissatisfied) < 10%.
Provide individual thermal comfort controls for at least 50% of individual occupant spaces. Group thermal comfort controls for all shared multi-occupant spaces.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, IEQ Credit: Thermal Comfort

title: Interior Lighting
intent: Promote occupants' productivity, comfort, and well-being by providing high-quality lighting.

requirements.unit: percent of individual occupants with personal controls
requirements.notes: For individual occupant spaces: provide individual lighting controls that enable occupants to adjust lighting to suit their individual tasks and preferences, for at least 90% of individual occupant spaces. Controls must allow at minimum on/off and light level adjustment.

requirements.option: Option 1: Shared space multi-zone controls (1 point)
requirements.notes: For shared multi-occupant spaces: provide multi-zone controls enabling occupants to adjust lighting to meet group needs and preferences. Lighting for any shared space must have at least three lighting levels (on, mid-level, off).

Choose at least four of eight strategies: luminance ratios, direct-only overhead fixtures with luminaire luminance limits, high CRI (>80) for 75% of total connected load, color rendering for specific applications, fixture shielding (>45 degree cutoff), ceiling-surface reflectance, furniture-surface reflectance, electric lighting for occupied spaces only.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, IEQ Credit: Interior Lighting

title: Daylight
intent: Connect building occupants with the outdoors, reinforce circadian rhythms, and reduce the use of electrical lighting by introducing daylight into the space.

requirements.unit: percent of regularly occupied floor area
requirements.notes: Demonstrate through annual computer simulations that sDA300/50% (spatial Daylight Autonomy: 300 lux for at least 50% of annual occupied hours) is achieved in the specified percentage of regularly occupied floor area. Additionally, Annual Sunlight Exposure (ASE1000,250h) must not exceed 10% of regularly occupied floor area; blinds may be modeled to demonstrate ASE compliance.

requirements.unit: percent of regularly occupied floor area
requirements.notes: Annual Sunlight Exposure (1000 lux for more than 250 occupied hours per year) must not exceed 10% of regularly occupied floor area. Spaces failing ASE must demonstrate adequate glare control (blinds, automated shading, etc.).

Demonstrate through illuminance calculations that 300 lux minimum is achieved for at least 75% of regularly occupied floor area at 9 AM and 3 PM on equinox, with a maximum of 3000 lux (to manage glare).

Demonstrate through measurement that 300 lux minimum is achieved for 75% of regularly occupied floor area. Measurements taken on a 10-foot grid at desk height (30 inches / 760 mm) between 9 AM and 3 PM.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, IEQ Credit: Daylight

title: Quality Views
intent: Give building occupants a connection to the natural outdoor environment by providing quality views.

requirements.option: Direct line of sight to vision glazing
requirements.unit: percent of regularly occupied floor area
requirements.notes: Achieve a direct line of sight to the outdoors via vision glazing for 75% of all regularly occupied floor area. View glazing in the 42-inch (1,067 mm) to 90-inch (2,286 mm) zone above finished floor qualifies. Views into interior atriums may be counted for up to 30% of the required area. Views must include at least two of the following: (1) flora, fauna, or sky; (2) movement; (3) objects at least 25 feet (7.5 m) from the exterior of the glazing.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, IEQ Credit: Quality Views

title: Acoustic Performance
intent: Provide workspaces and classrooms that promote occupants' well-being, productivity, and communications through effective acoustic design.

requirements.notes: Achieve maximum background noise levels from HVAC systems per 2011 ASHRAE Handbook Chapter 48 design criteria. Typical targets: open offices NC/RC 40, private offices NC/RC 35, conference rooms NC/RC 30, classrooms NC/RC 35.

Achieve minimum composite Sound Transmission Class (STC) ratings for wall, floor-ceiling, and door assemblies between spaces. Typical targets: conference room to corridor STC 50, office to office STC 45, classroom to corridor STC 50. Schools must also meet ANSI S12.60.
For classrooms with volume up to 10,000 cubic feet, meet a maximum reverberation time (T60) of 0.6 seconds. For classrooms with volume from 10,000 to 20,000 cubic feet, maximum T60 of 0.7 seconds.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, IEQ Credit: Acoustic Performance

title: Low-Emitting Materials
intent: Reduce concentrations of chemical contaminants that can damage air quality, human health, productivity, and the environment.

requirements.unit: percent of products meeting VOC limits per category
requirements.notes: Use the budget calculation method. Achieve threshold percentage of compliant products in the required number of categories: (1) interior paints and coatings, (2) adhesives and sealants, (3) flooring, (4) composite wood, (5) ceilings/walls/thermal/acoustic insulation, (6) furniture, (7) exterior applied products.
Adhesives, sealants, and sealant primers applied on site must meet the VOC content limits of South Coast Air Quality Management District (SCAQMD) Rule 1168.
Paints and coatings applied on site must meet VOC limits per Green Seal GS-11 (flat: 50 g/L, non-flat: 100 g/L) or SCAQMD Rule 1113.
Flooring products must be FloorScore certified or meet CDPH Standard Method v1.1 (California Section 01350) emission limits.

requirements.standard: CARB ATCM for formaldehyde / EPA TSCA Title VI
requirements.notes: Composite wood and agrifiber products must contain no added urea-formaldehyde resins. Laminating adhesives for field and shop applications must contain no added urea-formaldehyde resins.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, IEQ Credit: Low-Emitting Materials

title: Heat Island Reduction
intent: Minimize effects on microclimates and human and wildlife habitats by reducing heat islands.

requirements.notes: Use any combination of non-roof strategies (high-reflectance paving SRI >= 29, open-grid pavement, shade from structures/trees/vegetated pergolas within 10 years, underground parking) and roof strategies to meet the combined equation: (non-roof compliant area / total non-roof hardscape) + (high-SRI roof area / total roof area) >= 1.
For low-slope roof (slope <= 2:12): use roofing materials with an initial SRI of at least 82 (or 3-year aged SRI of at least 64) for a minimum of 75% of the roof surface.
For steep-slope roof (slope > 2:12): use roofing materials with an initial SRI of at least 39 (or 3-year aged SRI of at least 32) for a minimum of 75% of the roof surface.

Install a vegetated (green) roof for at least 75% of the roof area. A combination of high-SRI roofing and vegetated roof can be used with the weighted formula: (SRI roof area / 0.75) + (vegetated roof area / 0.75) >= total roof area.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, SS Credit: Heat Island Reduction

title: Building Product Disclosure and Optimization - Environmental Product Declarations
intent: Encourage the use of products and materials for which life-cycle information is available and that have environmentally, economically, and socially preferable life-cycle impacts.

requirements.notes: Use at least 20 different permanently installed products sourced from at least five different manufacturers that have: (a) Product-specific Type III EPD conforming to ISO 14025 and EN 15804 or ISO 21930, OR (b) USGBC-approved program EPDs. Industry-wide (generic) EPDs count as one-half product; product-specific EPDs count as one whole product.

Of the products with EPDs, demonstrate that at least 50% (by cost) show impact reduction below industry average in at least three of six impact categories: global warming potential, ozone depletion, acidification, eutrophication, photochemical ozone creation, and depletion of abiotic resources.

source_clause: LEED v4.1 BD+C, MR Credit: Building Product Disclosure and Optimization - Environmental Product Declarations
